translation: By the chargers [horses ridden by warriors] panting,
urdu: ان سرپٹ دوڑنے والے گھوڑوں کی قسم جو ہانپ اٹھتےہیں

translation: Then striking the sparks of fire,
notes: The analogy here is to the sparks arising when the hoofs of the racing horses strike a stony surface.
urdu: پھر (پتھروں پر نعل) مار کر آگ نکالتے ہیں
پھر صبح کو چھاپہ مارتے ہیں
پھر اس میں گرد اٹھاتے ہیں

translation: Then charging into the midst collectively therewith,
urdu: پھر اس وقت دشمن کی فوج میں جا گھستے ہیں

translation: Man certainly indeed is ungrateful to his Lord!
notes: This is the sad fact for the veracity of which the divine oath is taken as in Verses 1 to 5 above. The object on which the oath is taken appears to be a bandit raid on a locality in the early hours of the morning when the inhabitants are not fully awake and alert. The object corresponds to the depravity of man’s conduct of being ungrateful to his Creator. But the reader is cautioned that the objects on which divine oaths are taken in the Qur’aan fall in the category of mutashabihaat, the meanings of which are not very clear to the human mind. As such, in terms of [3:7], we should not delve deep into their interpretation beyond what is apparent. The oaths serve the purpose of stressing the veracity of the divine statements that follow these. The statements are the essence of the Book, and they are unambiguously put forth for necessary action by mankind. This Verse here is one of such clear divine statements.
urdu: کہ انسان اپنے پروردگار کا احسان ناشناس (اور ناشکرا) ہے

translation: And he certainly indeed bears witness on that (that he is ungrateful);
urdu: اور وہ اس سے آگاہ بھی ہے

translation: And he is certainly indeed extremely fond of wealth.
urdu: وہ تو مال سے سخت محبت کرنے والا ہے

arabic: ۞أَفَلَا يَعۡلَمُ إِذَا بُعۡثِرَ مَا فِي ٱلۡقُبُورِ
transliteration: Afala yaAAlamu itha buAAthira ma fee alquboori
translation: Does he not know that when the contents of the graves are taken out and examined,
urdu: کیا وہ اس وقت کو نہیں جانتا کہ جو (مردے) قبروں میں ہیں وہ باہر نکال لیے جائیں گے

translation: And that which was in (human) minds recovered,
urdu: اور جو (بھید) دلوں میں ہیں وہ ظاہر کر دیئے جائیں گے

translation: Their Lord shall have been well-aware of them, that Day?
notes: Man’s ungratefulness to Allah and his intense love for worldly wealth have never been hidden from Allah! It is well- recorded within his own self, and it will be brought out on Judgment Day as evidence against him.
urdu: بےشک ان کا پروردگار اس روز ان سے خوب واقف ہوگا
